context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements server-side tenant theme injection as an inline style tag (not client-only), serializes overrides as --store- CSS custom properties, and sanitizes tenant values to prevent XSS.

checklist.name: Server-side injection
checklist.description: Tenant theme overrides are applied in server-side middleware or server rendering logic — not exclusively in a client-side `useEffect` or `componentDidMount`

checklist.name: Inline style tag in head
checklist.description: The server renders tenant overrides as an inline `<style>` tag (e.g., `res.locals.themeOverrides` or equivalent) placed in the `<head>` of the HTML document

checklist.name: store- variable prefix
checklist.description: Serialized CSS overrides use the `--store-` prefix (e.g., `--store-color-brand-primary: #e63946;`) matching the generated token naming convention

checklist.name: Overrides scoped to :root
checklist.description: The injected inline style block scopes overrides to `:root { ... }` so they cascade to all components

checklist.name: Sanitization present
checklist.description: Code includes a sanitization or validation step that processes tenant theme values before they are injected into HTML

checklist.name: Allows color values
checklist.description: Sanitization accepts valid CSS color formats (hex like `#e63946`, or named colors) as permitted values

checklist.name: Allows rem/px lengths
checklist.description: Sanitization accepts rem and px length values (e.g., `1.0625rem`, `16px`) as permitted values

checklist.name: Allows border-radius values
checklist.description: Sanitization accepts border-radius values including pixel and `9999px` pill values

checklist.name: Rejects arbitrary strings
checklist.description: Sanitization rejects or strips values that are not colors, lengths, or border-radius — e.g., `url(...)`, `expression(...)`, multi-value strings with semicolons, or JavaScript

checklist.name: Tenant lookup from request
checklist.description: Middleware resolves the tenant from the incoming request (e.g., via hostname, subdomain, or header) before applying overrides
